title: Storage | Logos
description: Private, Secure, Decentralised Storage. Content-addressed file storage and retrieval with built-in privacy guarantees, built around the Logos Messaging Nim (LMN) node.
heading: Storage

sections.headline: Storage
sections.body: Decentralised file storage and retrieval using content identifiers.

items.title: LOGOS-STORAGE-MODULE
items.description: Logos module for decentralised file storage and retrieval.

status.label: Current Status
status.body: Logos Storage is integrated into the stack with a module API and a libstorage library for lower-level access and language bindings. The Logos Storage module is available within Logos Basecamp.

sections.title: Overview
sections.description: Files are addressed by content rather than by location. When you store a file, the network returns a CID — a content identifier derived from the file itself — and anyone with that CID can retrieve the file from whichever nodes hold it. There is no central server to depend on and no single host to fail. The usage is straightforward: store a file and get back a content identifier; provide a content identifier and get back the file. Storage works both inside the Logos App and from your own applications, through either the high-level module API or the lower-level libstorage library.

cards.label: Messaging
cards.title: Storage Module API
cards.description: The module API gives applications a clean interface for storing and retrieving content: initialise the module, start the node, upload a file to receive a CID, and download by CID. Streaming upload is available where you need finer control.

cards.label: Messaging
cards.title: libstorage
cards.description: For lower-level access or to build bindings for another language, libstorage exposes the underlying storage primitives directly.
